note: Explorer load-time fix (round 2). Two more functions still serialised dozens of RPC calls and stalled the page when the node was busy: loadLatestTxs (up to 31 getblockhash + 31 getblock + per-block gettransaction, all sequential — 60+ round trips) and loadMempool (up to 50 getrawtransaction calls sequential). Both now run in parallel via Promise.all with per-call .catch() so a single slow read no longer blocks the rest. The top-level refresh() now also (a) bails out immediately if loadStats failed (node offline — no point hammering the same dead RPC), and (b) runs loadBlocks / loadLatestTxs / loadMempool concurrently instead of sequentially. Net effect: cold open over a contended RPC drops from tens of seconds to a few seconds, and the offline banner appears instantly instead of after 45 s of stacked timeouts. Hard reload (Ctrl+Shift+R).
